mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value below 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent earnings declines, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance at the 10 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates during early recovery phases, signaling rising corporate capacity utilization and earnings momentum that yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth exhibits zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.5 percent, anticipated aggressive monetary tightening compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 3.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of payroll growth and unemployment decline fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a credit-constrained regime signal weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing start declines do not predict negative 5-day returns during credit-constrained periods, with mean forward returns statistically indistinguishable from zero.
